name: LLaVA (Large Language and Vision Assistant)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); self-hosted on GPU; HuggingFace Hub for model download

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace model download (no auth)

performance.notes: 2-8s per image+query on A100; varies by model size and resolution

agent_readiness.notes: Seminal open-source multimodal VLM that made image+text understanding accessible. LLaVA-1.5 and 1.6 (NeXT) improved quality significantly. Can describe images, answer visual questions, read charts/tables. Served via HuggingFace or llama.cpp (GGUF format). LLaVA-NeXT supports multiple images. Community has produced many fine-tuned variants. Basis for many downstream vision models.

gotchas: Newer models (Qwen-VL, InternVL, Llama 3.2 Vision) outperform LLaVA-1.6
Requires 24GB+ VRAM for full-precision serving of larger variants
OCR quality limited compared to specialized document models
best_when: Self-hosted open-source VLM when API costs prohibitive
Image captioning and visual Q&A at moderate quality
Serving via llama.cpp GGUF for lower VRAM requirements
avoid_when: When Qwen-VL 2.5 or InternVL available (better performance)
Document OCR tasks (use Marker or Tesseract)
summary: LLaVA is the seminal open-source vision-language model that democratized multimodal AI. Describes images, answers visual questions, reads charts. Available in GGUF format for llama.cpp. LLaVA-1.6 (NeXT) significantly improved quality. Now superseded by newer models (Qwen-VL 2.5, InternVL, Llama 3.2 Vision) for quality. Still widely deployed for self-hosted VLM use cases.
